title: Embargoed forward CV with variance-penalized model selection
summary: Pit-stop labels are event-time dependent, so grouped CV by race can still look optimistic if train folds contain neighboring laps/races that leak future regime information.
rationale: Pit-stop labels are event-time dependent, so grouped CV by race can still look optimistic if train folds contain neighboring laps/races that leak future regime information. Purged/embargoed CV is designed for overlapping-label settings, and the recent tire state-space paper also evaluates with rolling-origin splits rather than random grouping.
implementation_hint: Replace model selection CV with forward Year/Race ordering plus a small embargo around each validation race; score each candidate by mean AUC minus a fold-variance penalty, then keep the old CV only as a secondary diagnostic.
expected_effect: May not raise local CV, but should reduce false-positive research directions and improve public LB transfer for sub-0.001 AUC tweaks.
risk: Could lower headline local CV and slow iteration if the competition split is less shift-heavy than expected.
